item.title: Live reader Q&A with Isabel Berwick — is university worth it?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Isabel Berwick, the FT’s Working It editor, now

item.title: How capable is AI? It depends what you’re worried about
item.description: Metrics for dangerous success and dangerous failure should be interpreted differently

item.title: US energy regulator sets ultimatum for data centres
item.description: Last month FERC gave grid operators 60 days to justify or revise how they connect ‘large loads’ — or the regulator will make the decision for them

item.title: FirstFT: OpenAI proposes US government stake
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Asia’s biggest IPO this year, and Google loses appeal against record €4bn EU competition fine

item.title: How AI could make markets worse
item.description: Heed the lessons of information economics

item.title: Yet another ‘quant tremor’ strikes systematic investors
item.description: The garbage rally roils quantitative investment strategies

item.title: Beijing plane crash pilot suffered from ‘insomnia and anxiety’, say police
item.description: Pilot who was living alone after divorce had made ‘repeated references’ to suicide in his diary, according to authorities

item.title: Trump blocks long-term renewal of North America trade pact
item.description: Washington will hold annual reviews on terms of commerce with its biggest trading partners

item.title: Forvis Mazars and partner fined £600,000 by regulator
item.description: Group lambasted by FRC for ‘numerous, serious and pervasive failings’ in Studio Retail audit

item.title: Russian drone campaign mapped Nato air defence gaps, study finds
item.description: Nearly 150 UAV incursions into more than a dozen European countries were made over 19 months, IISS report says

item.title: PwC picks Canary Wharf for new London office
item.description: Accountancy firm is taking additional office space as it considers where to base its main London headquarters

item.title: Google loses appeal against record €4.1bn EU fine over Android
item.description: European Court of Justice decision marks a significant win for Brussels’ crackdown on Big Tech

item.title: AI’s big questions for humanity and Japan’s start-up mojo
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Quantum Systems weighs merger with kamikaze drone start-up Stark
item.description: German maker of unmanned surveillance aircraft says $1.2bn fundraising removed shareholder opposition to developing lethal weapons

item.title: Chinese energy group’s shares triple in Asia’s biggest IPO this year
item.description: China Resources New Energy raises $3.6bn amid heavy demand from retail investors

item.title: What are the rules on insider betting, really?
item.description: It’s more interesting than you might think

item.title: Chanel acquires luxury Parisian shirtmaker Charvet
item.description: Deal for revered brand follows earlier collaboration with Chanel creative director Matthieu Blazy

item.title: OpenAI proposes handing Trump administration 5% stake
item.description: Sam Altman’s start-up in early talks for a public ownership deal as political pressure rises

item.title: Bank of England to push ahead with plan to limit hedge fund leverage
item.description: Move to make gilt market more resilient has been criticised for potentially raising funding costs

item.title: Bending Spoons takes private equity public
item.description: The Italian tech group behind AOL is a good analogue for PE-backed software and tech roll-ups

item.title: HMRC’s clampdown on the corporate structure that saves City firms millions
item.description: UK tax authority has won a series of legal cases involving limited liability partnerships

item.title: ‘Let it rip’: how US egg producers gamed prices while blaming bird flu
item.description: Evidence amassed by prosecutors shows executives privately colluded to keep prices aloft while shifting focus for cause

item.title: EU weighs weaker data centre climate rules in win for Big Tech
item.description: Past proposals for strict rules on renewable energy certificates have been dropped after heavy lobbying, draft shows

item.title: Burnham’s team looks to revamp UK’s AI strategy
item.description: Focus is expected to shift to making technology work for local communities rather than US companies

item.title: UBS to trial US banking services in push for wealthy American clients
item.description: Swiss group plans to open accounts for staff as early as December to test pricing and products

item.title: How do we save the wilting new homes market?
item.description: Like it or not, the government will have to help developers before houses and rents become even more unaffordable

item.title: Americans prepare for most expensive July 4 celebration ever
item.description: Tariffs and war in Iran have kept food and gasoline prices high as country marks 250th birthday

item.title: How to lift Europe out of high-tech mediocrity
item.description: Red tape, not funding, is the real bottleneck for competitiveness

item.title: BAE’s next-generation combat jet lifted by defence funding boost
item.description: New technology is speeding up development and cutting costs of military equipment

item.title: Battery start-ups see ‘crazy’ demand to smooth power surges in data centres
item.description: Rapid growth of clusters of processors for AI training drives need for energy storage
